behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from the recommendation results regardless of their relevance score.
behaviors.justification: Failure is most likely at the module boundary between availability filtering and ranking merge. A unit test of only scoring or only filtering can miss a handoff bug where high-score out-of-stock items are reintroduced. Rejected System because behavior text is about catalog/status logic, not real OS/network/subprocess. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no real AI judgment or multi-agent chain is required.
behaviors.test_description: Wire catalog loader + availability filter + ranker with mixed stock states and high relevance out-of-stock items; assert final returned list contains zero out-of-stock products.

behaviors.description: The user preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is settings retrieval/interpretation flowing into filter logic. Single-function unit tests can miss schema/key mismatches between user settings and category filter modules. Rejected System: no real infra requirement in behavior text. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: deterministic preference rule.
behaviors.test_description: Use real in-process settings model/repository and recommendation filter pipeline; set blocked categories and assert none of those categories appear in output.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm ranks products that match the user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is primarily scoring/ranking logic inside the recommendation algorithm; controlled feature inputs to one ranking function are sufficient to expose failures. Rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on ranking rule itself, not cross-module handoff. Rejected Agentic because it does not require non-deterministic model judgment; deterministic scoring can be validated directly.
behaviors.test_description: Call ranking function with fixed candidate feature vectors where some items have strong recent-browsing match and others have zero browsing signal; assert matched items score/order above no-signal items.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page of results, with stable ordering — requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if the underlying data hasn't changed.
behaviors.justification: Stable pagination failures often occur when sorter + paginator + repository query ordering are wired together (e.g., missing deterministic tie-break). Unit tests of paginator alone can pass while integrated ordering drifts. Rejected System because in-process data source can reproduce this boundary failure without real network/subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: Execute recommendation query through full in-process pipeline with fixed dataset containing tie-score items; request page 2 twice with unchanged data and assert identical item IDs/order and correct offset window.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category the user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding a filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: This is a diversification constraint applied on top of ranked candidates, typically needing user-history signals plus reranking/diversity logic together. Unit-testing one function may miss failures where upstream candidate generation removes unseen categories before diversification. Rejected Agentic because requirement can be validated as deterministic business constraint, not model non-determinism.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end in-process recommendation assembly (history features + candidate set + diversification stage) for a user with narrow browse history; assert at least one final recommendation is from an unseen category.

behaviors.description: A new user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than an empty result set.
behaviors.justification: Cold-start fallback depends on interaction between history detection and fallback popular-items provider. Unit tests can miss wiring bugs where fallback path is never invoked. Rejected System because real OS/network behavior is not required by text. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because fallback rule is deterministic.
behaviors.test_description: Pass a user with empty history through recommendation service wired to popularity source module; assert non-empty output and that items correspond to popularity ranking path, not personalized path.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records the correct product ID, position in the list, and timestamp for each recommendation the user interacts with.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is between click event handler, ranking context, and persistence payload mapping. Unit tests of event object creation alone can miss position/timestamp field loss or mis-mapping at write boundary. Rejected System: unless behavior specified real queue/HTTP/DB infra issues, in-process persistence adapter is enough.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate served recommendation list and user click through tracking pipeline into storage adapter; assert persisted record contains exact product ID, displayed index, and timestamp from injected clock.

behaviors.description: The response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: This is a real performance/SLA behavior where OS scheduling, runtime, memory, and actual process execution matter; mocks/in-process micro-units can hide real latency regressions. Integration is insufficient for the stated failure boundary (<200ms under realistic load size). Rejected Agentic/Workflow because latency target is infrastructure/runtime performance, not model non-determinism or multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run a system-level benchmark in a real process with a 100k-product dataset and representative user state; measure end-to-end recommendation API latency and assert p95 (or specified metric) under 200ms.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased by the user, the recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests.
behaviors.justification: Needs interaction between purchase-event update path and subsequent ranking state read. Unit tests of ranking alone or event handler alone can miss stale-state propagation bugs. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real external infra; local module wiring catches the core failure.
behaviors.test_description: Generate initial recommendations, submit a purchase event for one recommended product, then request recommendations again via same service pipeline; assert purchased product rank drops (or is excluded per policy).

behaviors.description: The recommendation results are deterministic for the same user state — requesting recommendations twice with no intervening user activity returns the same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: Determinism failures usually emerge from combined effects of sorting ties, randomization seeds, and data retrieval order across modules. A unit test of sorter alone may miss nondeterminism introduced upstream/downstream. Rejected Agentic because behavior expects deterministic output for fixed state, not non-deterministic model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run full in-process recommendation generation twice for identical user/candidate state with no writes between calls; assert ranked product ID sequence is exactly identical.
